headline: Coding-agent tooling led the quarter as open-weights models pushed million-token context windows while browser-sandbox techniques broke out.
body: Technique-level rotation centered on the AI layer, workflow automation, and the developer stack of scoped skills, LSP, subagents, the PIV Loop, and prompt caching, all lagging but moving together. Open-weights coding models advanced as MiniMax M3 shipped million-token context with Anthropic and OpenAI API compatibility and multi-agent IDE features, and GLM 5.2 claimed top open-source status across multimodal generation [3][6]. Breakouts split between efficiency methods, hybrid strategy and quantization, and a cluster of new browser-isolation entrants including double iframe, Content Security Policy, srcdoc, and origin isolation. Agent reliability stayed in focus, with one talk documenting web agents that falsely claim live search while falling back on stale data, and another weighing AI agents managing passwords against a record Patch Tuesday [0][2].

cited_items.title: Your Agent's Biggest Lie: "I Searched the Web" — Rafael Levi, Bright Data
AI agents can manage your passwords. Should we let them? Plus: The biggest Patch Tuesday ever.
